briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In Florida heavy grass, switch to 30-lb braid with a 12-inch monofilament leader (Sufix Elite) for topwater — need to force fish out of hay-field grass or you'll lose them
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Prefers monofilament over braid for topwater because braid's zero stretch risks jerking the bait out of the fish's mouth when anglers instinctively jerk on the blowup. Mono's stretch provides forgiveness. Only exception is Florida heavy g...

KVD: 'Grassy lakes have 3 depth zones: Shallow flats (0-4ft) — topwater, spinnerbaits, weedless plastics for sight-feeding fish. Weed edges (4-8ft) — the sweet spot, bass ambush from the edge, use soft plastics, jigs, creature baits. Deeper transi...

Palaniuk: 'My low-light system: 1) topwater walker for aggressive fish and covering water, 2) jerkbait at 5-6 feet when bass wont surface, 3) weightless wacky-rigged stick bait as finesse to slow down for non-aggressive bass. Use all three early m...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
